theme: Light mode only. Clean, minimalist, modern, edge-to-edge.

visual_enhancers.borders: 1px solid border-slate-200. Very crisp, no soft gradients. Use sharp lines to define the grid layout.
visual_enhancers.shadows: Very subtle. Use shadow-sm for cards. Use shadow-[0_8px_30px_rgb(0,0,0,0.08)] for slide-in panels (Sheets) and Modals.
visual_enhancers.glassmorphism: Used only for sticky headers (e.g. top date bar in planning) and slide-in panel overlays: bg-white/80 backdrop-blur-md border-b border-slate-200. Never use pure transparent backgrounds for content overlays.
visual_enhancers.spacing: Dense but clear. Use p-4 to p-6 for cards. In the planning view, padding should be minimal (p-1 or p-2) to maximize screen real estate.

grids_and_layouts.global_layout: Edge-to-edge (h-screen w-screen). Left sidebar (w-64 or w-20 collapsed) with standard navigation. Main content area fills remaining space with its own scroll (overflow-auto).
grids_and_layouts.dashboard_kpis: grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-6. F-pattern layout. Top-left for most critical metric (e.g., Occupancy % or RevPAR).
grids_and_layouts.planning_view: A complex CSS grid. Left column (sticky): Room numbers/types. Top row (sticky): Dates (1-30). Cells represent a day/room intersection. Use 'border-collapse' style layout for the cells to create a clean spreadsheet feel. Reservations are positioned as horizontal colored bars spanning multiple cells.
grids_and_layouts.slide_in_panel: Right-aligned Sheet (Shadcn). Width should be large (w-[600px] or sm:max-w-xl). Inside, use a Tabs component (Infos, Client, Facturation, Historique) to manage dense data.

component_setup.guidelines: Do NOT use HTML native inputs for dates or selects. Use Shadcn customized components. Override default shadcn radii to 'rounded-md' (0.375rem) or 'rounded-sm' (0.125rem) for a sharper, more technical SaaS look instead of overly pill-shaped. No cards everywhere; favor 'cardless' flat sections separated by 1px borders for data-heavy views.

motion.interactions: Hover states on all buttons and table rows (hover:bg-slate-50). Drag and drop must have a subtle scale-up effect (`scale: 1.02`) and shadow while dragging.
motion.transitions: Use `transition-colors duration-200` for all text and background color changes. Do NOT use `transition-all`. Sheet should slide in smoothly using default Shadcn Framer Motion/CSS transitions.

accessibility.contrast: Maintain strictly >4.5:1 for standard text. Channel colored bars must have high-contrast text (e.g., white text on dark blue, dark slate text on bright yellow).
accessibility.testing: All interactive elements MUST include a `data-testid` attribute in kebab-case. Example: `data-testid="btn-new-reservation"`, `data-testid="planning-cell-room-101-day-15"`.

images.description: Clean, luxury hotel room (White mattress, sectional sofa)

3d_libraries_and_charts.strategy: No 3D elements needed as this is a high-density operational PMS. Use `recharts` for 2D data visualization in the 'Rapports' and 'Dashboard' sections.
3d_libraries_and_charts.charts: Line charts for RevPAR/ADR trends over time. Bar charts for channel distribution. Donut charts for Occupancy %. Keep colors tied to the channel palette or monochrome blue/slate for financial data.

instructions_to_main_agent: CRITICAL: The entire application MUST be built in French.
CRITICAL: Do not use ANY emojis in the UI or codebase. Use strictly `lucide-react` icons.

CRITICAL: Flowtym is a PMS. The most important view is the 'Planning principal'. It must feel like an interactive Excel sheet—dense, fast, grid-based, with absolutely positioned colored bars representing stays. Use a frozen left column for rooms and frozen top row for dates.
Data structure for planning bars should include start_date, end_date, room_id, guest_name, and channel (to map to the correct color).
Slide-in Panel (Sheet) must be used for Reservation Details to avoid navigating away from the planning board.
The Night Audit (Clôture) should be a sequential workflow (using a vertical progress indicator) to perform validation checks.
Forms must use Shadcn form elements, completely removing Vercel/Stripe default looks. Use the 'Swiss High-Contrast' structural approach.
Apply `data-testid` to all forms, tabs, inputs, and primary action buttons.
Do NOT use HTML `<input type='date'>`, use Shadcn's Popover + Calendar.
Only implement Light Theme. Hardcode light theme classes if necessary, ignoring dark mode (`dark:xyz`) utility classes.
UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES_FOR_MAIN_AGENT: Avoid the generic 'AI slop' aesthetic. Do not center-align the app. Do not use generic gradients.
Create depth through layered z-indexes and 1px crisp borders, not soft heavy drop shadows.
Never use generic AI emojis. Only professional SVGs or Lucide icons.
Components MUST use named exports (`export const ComponentName = ...`) and Pages MUST use default exports.
Apply micro-animations (hover, focus, active states) cleanly. Exclude `transform` from universal transitions.
Use `sonner` for all toast notifications (e.g., successful booking, payment processed).
Assume pure React + Tailwind JS files (not TSX). Write safe clean modular components.
Ensure the UI uses French terminologies accurately.
